previous_picks.rationale: ENPH has exceptional YTD momentum (+54.9%), supported by a 9% jump on the launch of its IQ9S-3P commercial microinverter and a Barclays upgrade [tikr.com]. Despite a high P/E of 51.76 and short interest of 24.43%, its product catalyst and strong recent momentum outweigh valuation concerns.

FSLR has a compelling P/E of 16.64 with +78% 1-year return, and although YTD is negative (-6.07%), strong 1-month momentum (+16.23%) and a cheap valuation relative to growth make it attractive. Web search did not yield new major catalysts but the AI-driven solar demand narrative supports the thesis.

MPC stands out with a +48.39% YTD and +46% 1-year return, driven by strong refining margins and Wells Fargo's recent price target hike. A P/E of 16.0 and a manageable 1-month dip of -7.29% provide a good entry point.
Valero delivers a stellar +44.5% YTD and +72% 1-year return, with a P/E of 17.27 and a solid dividend yield of 2.03%. Its refining focus benefits from tight supply and OPEC cuts supporting margins.

Baker Hughes has a +24.79% YTD and +54% 1-year return, with a P/E of 18.66 and analyst consensus of 'buy'. The oilfield services sector benefits from sustained drilling activity despite recent oil price pullbacks.

Brookfield Renewable offers a high dividend yield of 4.19% and strong news sentiment (+0.506), with moderate 1-month momentum (+4.57%). While YTD is slightly negative (-4.41%), it benefits from the AI-driven power demand narrative and a low-risk utility-like profile.

NextEra Energy shows steady performance with a +8.71% YTD and +23% 1-year return, along with a 2.87% dividend yield and low short interest (0.01%). Its utility model provides stability amid energy sector volatility.

EOG has a strong +23.28% YTD and a P/E of 12.79, making it a relatively cheap pure-play E&P stock. It benefits from OPEC production cuts that support oil prices, though recent oil price weakness is a headwind.

Shell has a P/E of 12.28, a 3.96% dividend yield, and positive news sentiment (+0.261). Recent pressure from falling oil prices and a buyback pause creates a dip-buying opportunity, supported by strong Q1 cash flow and the ARC Resources LNG deal [ts2.tech].

SSE has a +27% 1-year return, a P/E of 22.14, and a dividend yield of 2.95%, with a recent UK-Japan investment agreement backing its growth. The stock offers defensive utility exposure with renewables upside.
